M0_Setup.name: Create database schema (users, subscriptions, transactions)
M0_Setup.description: SQL migrations برای users، subscriptions، transactions tables
testing: Connect to DB and list tables
M0_Setup.code_template:
-- migrations/001_init_schema.sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id BIGINT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  username VARCHAR(255),
  trial_start_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  trial_end_at TIMESTAMP,
  is_trial_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_user_id ON users(user_id);
M0_Setup.commit_message:
T03: Create database schema (users, subscriptions, transactions)

- Write migrations/001_init_schema.sql
- Create users table with trial fields
- Create subscriptions table with tier column
- Create transactions table for payment tracking
- Create all necessary indexes
- Run migrations successfully

Part of M0 milestone

M0_Setup.name: Create database schema (routes, queues, schedules)
M0_Setup.description: SQL migrations برای routes، post_queue tables
testing: Test insert sample route and queue
M0_Setup.code_template:
-- migrations/002_post_and_schedule.sql
CREATE TABLE routes (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id BIGINT REFERENCES users(id),
  source_channel_id BIGINT,
  target_channel_id BIGINT,
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE TABLE post_queue (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  route_id BIGINT REFERENCES routes(id),
  message_id_in_source BIGINT,
  source_date TIMESTAMP,
  status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'pending',
  retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  last_error TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
M0_Setup.commit_message:
T04: Create database schema (routes, post_queue)

- Write migrations/002_post_and_schedule.sql
- Create routes table with user reference
- Create post_queue table for message tracking
- Setup foreign key constraints
- Create all performance indexes
- Test with sample data

Part of M0 milestone

M0_Setup.name: Setup Rubpy client and async bot skeleton
M0_Setup.description: Rubpy client wrapper و bot entry point
M0_Setup.code_template:
# src/bot/main.py
import asyncio
from rubpy import Client

async def main():
    await init_db()
    client = Client()
    
    @client.on_message_receive()
    async def handle_message(user_id, message):
        logger.info(f"Message from {user_id}: {message}")
    
    await client.run()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
M0_Setup.commit_message:
T05: Setup Rubpy client and async bot skeleton

- Create src/bot/main.py with entry point
- Create src/bot/client.py wrapper
- Setup async event loop
- Implement basic message handler
- Integrate logging
- Document Rubpy API usage

Part of M0 milestone

before_starting: 3. Read task description in this JSON
4. Update EXECUTION_TRACKER: change to 🔄 In Progress
5. Create git branch: git checkout -b T##-short-name
during_development: 4. Include T## in every commit message

commit_message_format.complete:
Complete T## - task name

- Accomplishment 1
- Accomplishment 2

Part of M# milestone
Tests: ✅ passed
Issues: None
Risks: None

tracking.update_on_start: Change T## status to 🔄 In Progress
tracking.update_on_finish: Change T## status to ✅ Done، add date
tracking.log_issues: If bug found during task، log in EXECUTION_TRACKER
tracking.log_risks: If risk identified، log in EXECUTION_TRACKER
tracking.next_step: Check T## dependency for next task

M0_M1_M2_M3.code: All src/ folders complete، tested، documented
M0_M1_M2_M3.database: Migrations run، all tables created، indexes in place
M0_M1_M2_M3.tests: Unit tests for all services، integration tests for core flows

important: 🔴 NO ORM - Use asyncpg with raw SQL
